items.title: Middle East crisis live: US talks with Iran set to continue despite Iranian walkout
items.standfirst: Negotiations due to continue for rest of week in Switzerland after tense start as Iranians protest against threat from Donald TrumpIran hails ‘progress’ as first day of talks with US conclude after shaky startIn a statement, the Swiss foreign ministry said: “The Swiss facilitator welcomes the constructive progress made during the intensive diplomatic talks that continued throughout the night of 21-22 June at the Burgenstock between the mediators, Iran and the United States”.The ministry added that the roadmap agreed “creates the conditions for the immediate resumption of new technical discussions”. Continue reading...
items.content: Negotiations due to continue for rest of week in Switzerland after tense start as Iranians protest against threat from Donald TrumpIran hails ‘progress’ as first day of talks with US conclude after shaky startIn a statement, the Swiss foreign ministry said: “The Swiss facilitator welcomes the constructive progress made during the intensive diplomatic talks that continued throughout the night of 21-22 June at the Burgenstock between the mediators, Iran and the United States”.The ministry added that the roadmap agreed “creates the conditions for the immediate resumption of new technical discussions”. Continue reading...

items.title: First round of US-Iran talks ends with encouraging progress, mediators say
items.standfirst: The US-Iran talks began on Sunday in Switzerland, after last week's agreement, which includes a commitment to reach a final deal within 60 days.
items.content: The US-Iran talks began on Sunday in Switzerland, after last week's agreement, which includes a commitment to reach a final deal within 60 days.

items.title: U.S., Iranian negotiators meet; Trump threatens Iran over Hezbollah
items.standfirst: Their appearance was a rare face-to-face meeting between U.S. and Iranian officials as they launched a 60-day sprint to negotiate over the fate of Iran's nuclear program.
items.content: Their appearance was a rare face-to-face meeting between U.S. and Iranian officials as they launched a 60-day sprint to negotiate over the fate of Iran's nuclear program.

items.title: US, Iran agree on ‘roadmap’ towards final deal in Switzerland talks
items.standfirst: Technical talks to continue in the resort of Burgenstock after progress on issues of Strait of Hormuz and Lebanon.
items.content: Technical talks to continue in the resort of Burgenstock after progress on issues of Strait of Hormuz and Lebanon.

items.title: Iran hails ‘progress’ as first day of talks with US conclude after shaky start
items.standfirst: Mediators Pakistan and Qatar issue statement saying talks will run for rest of the week, as fighting in Lebanon continues to threaten dealIran’s foreign minister has declared “progress” after the first day of talks between high-ranking officials from Washington and Tehran ended in Switzerland, despite a tense opening marked by Donald Trump threats to restart attacks.A joint statement from mediators Qatar and Pakistan said the ⁠US and Iran agreed to a roadmap towards⁠ a final deal within 60 days. Technical talks between lower-ranked officials ​will continue for the rest of the week, according to the statement, with fighting between Israel and Iran-backed Hezbollah in Lebanon at the top of the agenda. Continue reading...
items.content: Mediators Pakistan and Qatar issue statement saying talks will run for rest of the week, as fighting in Lebanon continues to threaten dealIran’s foreign minister has declared “progress” after the first day of talks between high-ranking officials from Washington and Tehran ended in Switzerland, despite a tense opening marked by Donald Trump threats to restart attacks.A joint statement from mediators Qatar and Pakistan said the ⁠US and Iran agreed to a roadmap towards⁠ a final deal within 60 days. Technical talks between lower-ranked officials ​will continue for the rest of the week, according to the statement, with fighting between Israel and Iran-backed Hezbollah in Lebanon at the top of the agenda. Continue reading...

items.title: Middle East live: US, Iranian negotiators make progress on Lebanon, Hormuz after ‘encouraging’ start
items.standfirst: Iran and the US agreed on Monday to set up communication lines to keep the vital Strait of Hormuz open and end fighting in Lebanon, mediators said, after "encouraging" talks in Switzerland over the weekend. Follow our live coverage for all the latest updates.
items.content: Iran and the US agreed on Monday to set up communication lines to keep the vital Strait of Hormuz open and end fighting in Lebanon, mediators said, after "encouraging" talks in Switzerland over the weekend. Follow our live coverage for all the latest updates.

items.content: The first round of US-Iran talks has ended with both sides agreeing on a roadmap towards a final deal.

items.content: Trump threatens new attacks as U.S. and Iranian negotiators meet; at least 70 major fires burning in western U.S.

items.title: US-Iran talks strained as Trump threats spark Iranian walkout
items.standfirst: Talks expected to continue for rest of the week despite disruption caused by US president’s threat to bomb Iran and kidnap negotiating teamHigh-stakes talks between the US and Iran are expected to continue for the rest of the week in Switzerland, after a tense start that saw Iranian negotiators walk out in protest at a stream of threats issued by Donald Trump on social media.The US president had threatened to bomb Iran and even to kidnap the Iranian negotiating team unless the strait of Hormuz was reopened, forcing mediators Qatar and Pakistan to continue negotiations in the background. Continue reading...
items.content: Talks expected to continue for rest of the week despite disruption caused by US president’s threat to bomb Iran and kidnap negotiating teamHigh-stakes talks between the US and Iran are expected to continue for the rest of the week in Switzerland, after a tense start that saw Iranian negotiators walk out in protest at a stream of threats issued by Donald Trump on social media.The US president had threatened to bomb Iran and even to kidnap the Iranian negotiating team unless the strait of Hormuz was reopened, forcing mediators Qatar and Pakistan to continue negotiations in the background. Continue reading...

items.title: At least 70 major fires burning in western U.S.
items.standfirst: At least 70 major fires are burning in several states, fueled by extreme heat, and gusty winds. Jonathan Vigliotti reports.
items.content: At least 70 major fires are burning in several states, fueled by extreme heat, and gusty winds. Jonathan Vigliotti reports.

items.title: Trump threatens new attacks as U.S. and Iranian negotiators meet
items.standfirst: President Trump is threatening further attacks on Iran, even as Vice President JD Vance, in Switzerland, hails progress at talks aimed at securing peace. Taurean Small reports.
items.content: President Trump is threatening further attacks on Iran, even as Vice President JD Vance, in Switzerland, hails progress at talks aimed at securing peace. Taurean Small reports.

items.title: Switzerland talks enter tough phase after Trump renews threats against Iran
items.standfirst: US Vice President JD Vance said Sunday there was a chance to “turn over a new leaf” with Iran as negotiators met in Switzerland to build on last week’s interim agreement. But President Donald Trump’s renewed threats over Iran-backed Hezbollah and the Strait of Hormuz – and Israel's refusal to withdraw from a security zone in southern Lebanon – cast a long shadow over the fragile talks.
items.content: US Vice President JD Vance said Sunday there was a chance to “turn over a new leaf” with Iran as negotiators met in Switzerland to build on last week’s interim agreement. But President Donald Trump’s renewed threats over Iran-backed Hezbollah and the Strait of Hormuz – and Israel's refusal to withdraw from a security zone in southern Lebanon – cast a long shadow over the fragile talks.

items.title: What's the state of the Strait?
items.standfirst: US and Iranian officials have begun talks in Switzerland, just five days into a 60-day ceasefire agreement that already appears under strain. Tehran claims it has closed the Strait of Hormuz, citing the continued fighting in Lebanon, while Washington says the talks are focused on ending Iran's nuclear weapons ambitions and securing a lasting end to the conflict
items.content: US and Iranian officials have begun talks in Switzerland, just five days into a 60-day ceasefire agreement that already appears under strain. Tehran claims it has closed the Strait of Hormuz, citing the continued fighting in Lebanon, while Washington says the talks are focused on ending Iran's nuclear weapons ambitions and securing a lasting end to the conflict

items.standfirst: This live blog is now closed.Trump threatens fresh attacks on Iran as peace talks take place in SwitzerlandThere ⁠was and is ‘no restriction’ on ⁠Israeli ⁠soldiers ​to act to eliminate ⁠threats in Lebanon, and that troops would not withdraw from the security zone, ‌Israeli defence minister ‌Israel Katz said in a statement on Sunday, according to Reuters.Israeli strikes killed at ‌least 20 people in Lebanon ​on Saturday, Lebanon’s state news agency NNA reported, ⁠a day after a ​ceasefire with ​Iran-backed Hezbollah ​took effect ​after ‌months of ​escalating ​violence. Continue reading...

items.title: U.S. and Iran agree to 'roadmap' for final deal, mediators say
items.standfirst: Qatar and Pakistan said the talks made "encouraging progress" despite a rocky start after Trump made threats to "hit Iran very hard again" while the U.S. and Iranian negotiators met in Switzerland.
items.content: Qatar and Pakistan said the talks made "encouraging progress" despite a rocky start after Trump made threats to "hit Iran very hard again" while the U.S. and Iranian negotiators met in Switzerland.(Image credit: Nathan Howard)

items.title: Israeli strikes start again in Lebanon after several-hour lull, amid US-Iran talks
items.standfirst: Lebanon is at the centre of the ongoing US-Iran talks in Switzerland. Israel and Hezbollah continued to exchange fire in Lebanon despite the ceasefire concluded a few days ago. Iran answered by closing the Strait of Hormuz. On Sunday, fighting was reported to have stopped for almost 24 hours – a first since March 2 – before starting again. The latest from the ground by FRANCE 24 correpsondent in Lebanon, Renée Davis.
items.content: Lebanon is at the centre of the ongoing US-Iran talks in Switzerland. Israel and Hezbollah continued to exchange fire in Lebanon despite the ceasefire concluded a few days ago. Iran answered by closing the Strait of Hormuz. On Sunday, fighting was reported to have stopped for almost 24 hours – a first since March 2 – before starting again. The latest from the ground by FRANCE 24 correpsondent in Lebanon, Renée Davis.

items.title: U.S.-Iran negotiations end, technical talks will continue after Trump threats shake up summit
items.standfirst: The U.S. and Iran, along with mediating parties, established a road map for reaching a final deal within 60 days during Sunday’s talks in Switzerland, according to a joint statement from the mediating countries
items.content: The U.S. and Iran, along with mediating parties, established a road map for reaching a final deal within 60 days during Sunday’s talks in Switzerland, according to a joint statement from the mediating countries

items.title: Vance heads to Switzerland for Iran peace talks
items.standfirst: Vice President JD Vance departed for Switzerland Saturday to begin direct long-term peace negotiations with Iran. His trip comes as shipping in the Strait of Hormuz faced new disruptions after Iran said it was closing the waterway. Imtiaz Tyab reports.
items.content: Vice President JD Vance departed for Switzerland Saturday to begin direct long-term peace negotiations with Iran. His trip comes as shipping in the Strait of Hormuz faced new disruptions after Iran said it was closing the waterway. Imtiaz Tyab reports.

items.title: Iran closes Strait of Hormuz over ceasefire violations, state media reports
items.standfirst: Iran said it has closed the Strait of Hormuz over ceasefire violations, citing Israeli strikes on southern Lebanon, according to state media. U.S. Central Command released a statement stating that 55 merchant ships transited the strait on Saturday.
items.content: Iran said it has closed the Strait of Hormuz over ceasefire violations, citing Israeli strikes on southern Lebanon, according to state media. U.S. Central Command released a statement stating that 55 merchant ships transited the strait on Saturday.

items.title: Vance arrives in Switzerland for peace deal talks as Iran says Strait of Hormuz is closed
items.standfirst: Iran said Saturday that the Strait of Hormuz is closed, citing ceasefire violations after Israel continued deadly strikes in southern Lebanon overnight
items.content: Iran said Saturday that the Strait of Hormuz is closed, citing ceasefire violations after Israel continued deadly strikes in southern Lebanon overnight

items.title: Trump-backed populist wins tight Colombia presidential vote, sparking protests
items.standfirst: A flamboyant Trump-backed lawyer who's never held public office narrowly won Colombia's polarizing presidential runoff, swinging the country hard right and sparking violent protests.
items.content: A flamboyant Trump-backed lawyer who's never held public office narrowly won Colombia's polarizing presidential runoff, swinging the country hard right and sparking violent protests.

items.title: Far-right lawyer De La Espriella wins Colombia’s tight presidential race
items.standfirst: Far right lawyer Abelardo De La Espriella, who won backing of Donald Trump, seals narrow victory in run-off election.
items.content: Far right lawyer Abelardo De La Espriella, who won backing of Donald Trump, seals narrow victory in run-off election.

items.title: Trump-backed de la Espriella wins razor-tight Colombian presidential election
items.standfirst: Abelardo de la Espriella, a far-right lawyer and businessman who earned US President Donald Trump's support, on Sunday won Colombia's presidential runoff with 49.7 percent of the vote, beating his left-wing rival Ivan Cepeda with a one percent lead. Cepeda told supporters he would challenge results from more than 30,000 voting stations.
items.content: Abelardo de la Espriella, a far-right lawyer and businessman who earned US President Donald Trump's support, on Sunday won Colombia's presidential runoff with 49.7 percent of the vote, beating his left-wing rival Ivan Cepeda with a one percent lead. Cepeda told supporters he would challenge results from more than 30,000 voting stations.

items.content: Trump-loving political outsider Abelardo de la Espriella narrowly beats leftist continuity candidate

items.title: Colombia Election: Right-wing Abelardo de la Espriella wins, initial count shows
items.standfirst: Colombia wakes up to a sharp political turn to right as Abelardo de la Espriella's preliminary victory redraws the country's path on security, economy, and peace.
items.content: Colombia wakes up to a sharp political turn to right as Abelardo de la Espriella's preliminary victory redraws the country's path on security, economy, and peace.(Image credit: Ivan Valencia)

items.title: Far-right millionaire wins Colombia’s razor-tight presidential election
items.standfirst: Leftwing opponent alleges vote count irregularities after Trump-endorsed lawyer Abelardo de la Espriella secures narrow majorityThe Trump-admiring far-right millionaire lawyer and self-styled “outsider” Abelardo de la Espriella has won Colombia’s presidential runoff, defeating the leftwing senator Iván Cepeda.With 99.99% of ballots counted in the preliminary vote tally, De la Espriella had secured 12.96m votes, or 49.66%, just 250,830 more than Cepeda, who received 12.7m votes, or 48.7%. A further 1.6% of ballots were cast blank. Continue reading...
items.content: Leftwing opponent alleges vote count irregularities after Trump-endorsed lawyer Abelardo de la Espriella secures narrow majorityThe Trump-admiring far-right millionaire lawyer and self-styled “outsider” Abelardo de la Espriella has won Colombia’s presidential runoff, defeating the leftwing senator Iván Cepeda.With 99.99% of ballots counted in the preliminary vote tally, De la Espriella had secured 12.96m votes, or 49.66%, just 250,830 more than Cepeda, who received 12.7m votes, or 48.7%. A further 1.6% of ballots were cast blank. Continue reading...

items.title: De la Espriella wins razor-thin majority in Colombia's election, Cepeda calls for final count
items.standfirst: Conservative political outsider Abelardo de la Espriella won a razor-thin lead in Colombia's runoff presidential election on Sunday, a result that will be challenged in the coming days by the ruling party’s progressive candidate. Read our live blog to see how all the day's events unfolded.
items.content: Conservative political outsider Abelardo de la Espriella won a razor-thin lead in Colombia's runoff presidential election on Sunday, a result that will be challenged in the coming days by the ruling party’s progressive candidate. Read our live blog to see how all the day's events unfolded.

items.title: The last hours of Starmer?
items.standfirst: Ten years after Britain voted to leave the European Union, the UK could once again be facing a dramatic change in leadership. Prime Minister Keir Starmer is under mounting pressure amid growing speculation about his political future, with reports suggesting support within his own party is weakening and talk of a possible leadership contest gathering pace.
items.content: Ten years after Britain voted to leave the European Union, the UK could once again be facing a dramatic change in leadership. Prime Minister Keir Starmer is under mounting pressure amid growing speculation about his political future, with reports suggesting support within his own party is weakening and talk of a possible leadership contest gathering pace.

items.title: Pressure mounts on UK Prime Minister Starmer amid reports he could resign within days
items.standfirst: Embattled UK Prime Minister Keir Starmer was reflecting on the “political realities” facing his leadership, a senior minister said Sunday, as media reports suggested he could resign within days. The speculation follows rival Andy Burnham’s decisive by-election victory, which has strengthened expectations that the Labour heavyweight will challenge Starmer for the party leadership and premiership.
items.content: Embattled UK Prime Minister Keir Starmer was reflecting on the “political realities” facing his leadership, a senior minister said Sunday, as media reports suggested he could resign within days. The speculation follows rival Andy Burnham’s decisive by-election victory, which has strengthened expectations that the Labour heavyweight will challenge Starmer for the party leadership and premiership.

items.title: Trump says several people arrested for alleged vandalism of Reflecting Pool
items.standfirst: President Trump claims the problems with the Reflecting Pool in Washington are due to vandalism.
items.content: President Trump claims the problems with the Reflecting Pool in Washington are due to vandalism.

items.title: Trump says vandals sabotaged Reflecting Pool, Olympian arrested
items.standfirst: President Trump is accusing vandals of sabotaging the $14 million renovation on the Lincoln Memorial Reflecting Pool. So far, five people have been arrested, including three-time olympian and cyclist David Hearn, who was taken into custody after touching the pool's detached liner.
items.content: President Trump is accusing vandals of sabotaging the $14 million renovation on the Lincoln Memorial Reflecting Pool. So far, five people have been arrested, including three-time olympian and cyclist David Hearn, who was taken into custody after touching the pool's detached liner.

items.title: Trump says repairs to algae-plagued Reflecting Pool will begin immediately
items.standfirst: Despite a multi-million dollar renovation, the Washington DC landmark has peeling paint and algae. Trump claims vandals are partly to blame.
items.content: Despite a multi-million dollar renovation, the Washington DC landmark has peeling paint and algae. Trump claims vandals are partly to blame.

items.title: Trump says repair work to begin ‘immediately’ on beleaguered reflecting pool
items.standfirst: Algae blooms and peeling paint mar $14.2m renovation as president claims pool has been ‘seriously vandalized’Repair work will begin “immediately” at the troubled Lincoln Memorial reflecting pool in Washington DC, Donald Trump said on Sunday, after suggesting the pool would need to be drained and blaming alleged “vandals” for the disruption.The reflecting pool has been plagued by algae blooms and peeling paint following the controversial recent renovation efforts for America’s 250th anniversary celebrations next month. Continue reading...
items.content: Algae blooms and peeling paint mar $14.2m renovation as president claims pool has been ‘seriously vandalized’Repair work will begin “immediately” at the troubled Lincoln Memorial reflecting pool in Washington DC, Donald Trump said on Sunday, after suggesting the pool would need to be drained and blaming alleged “vandals” for the disruption.The reflecting pool has been plagued by algae blooms and peeling paint following the controversial recent renovation efforts for America’s 250th anniversary celebrations next month. Continue reading...

items.standfirst: The president posted on Truth Social claiming vandals slashed the pool's lining and poured chemicals into the water, saying arrests have been made. He provided no evidence for his claims.
items.content: The president posted on Truth Social claiming vandals slashed the pool's lining and poured chemicals into the water, saying arrests have been made. He provided no evidence for his claims.(Image credit: Mark Schiefelbein)

clusters.title: China sanctions US tech giants weeks after Pentagon blacklisted Chinese firms
clusters.neutral_headline: China sanctions US tech giants weeks after Pentagon blacklisted Chinese firms
bullet_summary: China on Monday announced sanctions on 10 American military-related companies in response to a recent U

items.title: China sanctions US tech giants weeks after Pentagon blacklisted Chinese firms
items.standfirst: China's commerce ministry on Monday imposed sanctions on 10 US tech companies, blocking them from exporting “dual-use” items, which have military and non-military applications, to the US. The companies barred from doing business with the US include drone makers and some involved in rare earths. Beijing's move comes weeks after the Pentagon slapped similar restrictions on Chinese companies.
items.content: China's commerce ministry on Monday imposed sanctions on 10 US tech companies, blocking them from exporting “dual-use” items, which have military and non-military applications, to the US. The companies barred from doing business with the US include drone makers and some involved in rare earths. Beijing's move comes weeks after the Pentagon slapped similar restrictions on Chinese companies.

items.title: China hits back at U.S. sanctions restricting its exports to American defense firms
items.standfirst: China on Monday announced sanctions on 10 American military-related companies in response to a recent U.S. move that bars some leading Chinese tech companies from defense contracts.
items.content: China on Monday announced sanctions on 10 American military-related companies in response to a recent U.S. move that bars some leading Chinese tech companies from defense contracts.(Image credit: Mark Schiefelbein)

items.content: Salah led Egypt to their first victory in the country's 92-year tournament history against New Zealand.

clusters.title: Cape Verde fight back for second World Cup draw 2-2 against Uruguay
clusters.neutral_headline: Cape Verde fight back for second World Cup draw 2-2 against Uruguay

items.title: Cape Verde fight back for second World Cup draw 2-2 against Uruguay
items.standfirst: Uruguay could need a win over Spain next weekend to avoid a second consecutive FIFA World Cup group-stage exit.
items.content: Uruguay could need a win over Spain next weekend to avoid a second consecutive FIFA World Cup group-stage exit.

items.title: World Cup 2026: Cape Verde fight back to earn second draw against Uruguay
items.standfirst: Newcomers Cape Verde fought back against two-time champions Uruguay in a 2-2 draw to clinch a second point at their maiden World Cup and maintain their unbeaten run, just days after causing the first shock of the tournament by holding favourites Spain to a goalless stalemate.
items.content: Newcomers Cape Verde fought back against two-time champions Uruguay in a 2-2 draw to clinch a second point at their maiden World Cup and maintain their unbeaten run, just days after causing the first shock of the tournament by holding favourites Spain to a goalless stalemate.

clusters.title: Leading Lebanese conservationist dies after Israeli airstrike on her home
clusters.neutral_headline: Lebanese turtle conservationist Mona Khalil killed by Israeli strike
bullet_summary: Mona Khalil, who had refused to leave the beach she had spent years protecting, died after several days in hospital, an environmental group said
Reported by NPR News, BBC World

items.title: Leading Lebanese conservationist dies after Israeli airstrike on her home
items.standfirst: Mona Khalil died Friday after an Israeli airstrike hit her beachside home two weeks ago. She's credited with creating a conservation movement in southern Lebanon to protect sea turtle nesting grounds.
items.content: Mona Khalil died Friday after an Israeli airstrike hit her beachside home two weeks ago. She's credited with creating a conservation movement in southern Lebanon to protect sea turtle nesting grounds.(Image credit: Jihad Seqlawi)

items.title: Lebanese turtle conservationist Mona Khalil killed by Israeli strike

items.content: Mona Khalil, who had refused to leave the beach she had spent years protecting, died after several days in hospital, an environmental group said.

clusters.title: Serena Williams will return to singles tennis at Wimbledon, as a wild card
clusters.neutral_headline: Serena Williams will return to singles tennis at Wimbledon, as a card

items.title: Serena Williams will return to singles tennis at Wimbledon, as a wild card
items.standfirst: Serena Williams recently returned to competition​ in doubles after nearly four years away from professional tennis.
items.content: Serena Williams recently returned to competition​ in doubles after nearly four years away from professional tennis.

items.title: Serena Williams handed Wimbledon singles wildcard in grand slam comeback
items.standfirst: Tennis legend Serena Williams will make a surprise return to Wimbledon singles after being awarded the tournament's final wildcard on Sunday, setting up one of the biggest comebacks of this year's championships. Williams has not played singles at Wimbledon since 2022 and has mostly been absent from competition for the past four years.
items.content: Tennis legend Serena Williams will make a surprise return to Wimbledon singles after being awarded the tournament's final wildcard on Sunday, setting up one of the biggest comebacks of this year's championships. Williams has not played singles at Wimbledon since 2022 and has mostly been absent from competition for the past four years.

clusters.title: Bolivian president declares state of emergency and deploys military to quell anti-government protests
clusters.neutral_headline: Bolivian president declares state of emergency
bullet_summary: Reported by Guardian World, BBC World

items.title: Bolivian president declares state of emergency and deploys military to quell anti-government protests
items.standfirst: Bulldozers sent in to clear roadblocks that have stifled the country as farmers and Indigenous groups protest against conservative president Bolivia’s president declared a state of emergency on Saturday and deployed soldiers and bulldozers to raze anti-government roadblocks that have paralysed the country.For more than six weeks, unions, Indigenous groups and coca farmers have marched through cities and blocked roads across the country with rubble, logs and debris in protest against the conservative government. Continue reading...
items.content: Bulldozers sent in to clear roadblocks that have stifled the country as farmers and Indigenous groups protest against conservative president Bolivia’s president declared a state of emergency on Saturday and deployed soldiers and bulldozers to raze anti-government roadblocks that have paralysed the country.For more than six weeks, unions, Indigenous groups and coca farmers have marched through cities and blocked roads across the country with rubble, logs and debris in protest against the conservative government. Continue reading...

items.title: Bolivian president declares state of emergency
items.standfirst: The move comes after weeks of anti-government protests that have caused a shortage of basic goods in Bolivia.
items.content: The move comes after weeks of anti-government protests that have caused a shortage of basic goods in Bolivia.
